syncActions.action: Updated steps + assertions to reflect new search/facet popup UI
syncActions.before: Steps reference 'select existing saved address from dropdown/list'; assertions reference 'dropdown'; Cross_Layer_Checks reference 'GET /account/addresses'
syncActions.after: Steps updated to reference address selection popup with keyword search input (data-test-id='search-keyword-input') and 3 facet dropdowns; assertions updated to [DOM] popup visible with search + facets; Cross_Layer_Checks updated to [API] currentCustomerAddresses query returns 200 + errors[] is empty. References appended with 'Synced: VCST-4710 (2026-04-23)'. Automation_Status: synced.
syncActions.note: Legacy 'dropdown/list' language pre-dates new server-side search popup. The feature shipped a modal with table + search + facets, not a dropdown. CHK-003 tested the old pattern.

syncActions.action: Updated Cross_Layer_Checks from legacy REST endpoint to new GraphQL query

syncActions.after: Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] currentOrganizationAddresses query returns org addresses with correct scope (not personal); errors[] is empty. References appended with 'Synced: VCST-4710 (2026-04-23)'. Automation_Status: synced.
syncActions.note: Org address loading now uses currentOrganizationAddresses GraphQL query (PR #129 + PR #2219). REST GET /account/addresses is not the primary address-fetch path for the popup.

syncActions.action: Added missing Cross_Layer_Checks for new GraphQL query
syncActions.before: Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] getCart or checkout query returns pre-selected default address
syncActions.after: Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] currentCustomerAddresses query called on popup open; pre-selected address matches address returned with isFavorite=true or isDefault=true; errors[] is empty. References appended 'Synced: VCST-4710 (2026-04-23)'. Automation_Status: synced.
syncActions.note: Pre-selection behavior now depends on composable default sort (lastName:asc for personal; isFavorite:desc for org) per PR #2219. This is a new behavioral invariant not covered in the original cross-layer check.

syncActions.action: Updated steps to use new popup interaction pattern; updated assertions for search UI
syncActions.before: Step: 'click Change address or address search/selector control'; no mention of 3-facet panel
syncActions.after: Steps now reference opening the address selection popup; using keyword search input and facet dropdowns to locate address; assertions include [DOM] 3 facet dropdowns visible (Country/State/City) and [DOM] search input accessible. References appended 'Synced: VCST-4710 (2026-04-23)'. Automation_Status: synced.
syncActions.note: CHK-059 was written before the popup had search/facets. The 'Change address' control now opens the full modal with search + 3 facets. Steps needed to reflect the new popup structure.

syncActions.action: Updated from client-side Show More pattern to server-side cursor pagination via currentCustomerAddresses
syncActions.before: Steps: click 'Show more' button; Cross_Layer_Checks: 'addresses query returns paginated result; second page returns additional items'
syncActions.after: Steps reference cursor-based pagination (after/first args); Cross_Layer_Checks updated to [API] currentCustomerAddresses called with after cursor on pagination action; second call returns new items; errors[] is empty. References appended 'Synced: VCST-4710 (2026-04-23)'. Automation_Status: synced.
syncActions.note: Pagination is now server-side via GraphQL cursor (paginationMode='server' in select-address-modal.vue). 'Show more' language is still plausible UI label but the underlying contract changed. The SEED REQUIRED note on OQ-5 retained.

syncActions.before: Tests me.contact.addresses[] via plain contact query — plain address list, no search/facet args
syncActions.after: Retained as-is: GQL-046 tests schema coverage of me.contact.addresses[], which uses the legacy connection. This is still a valid read-only schema coverage test. The legacy field is NOT removed (BA report confirms it's marked for future deprecation only). References appended 'Reviewed: VCST-4710 (2026-04-23)'. Automation_Status: unchanged.
syncActions.note: Legacy contact.addresses used here for plain field access, not for keyword/facet search. BA report explicitly states: legacy field used for 'get all addresses' remains VALID. No update needed.

syncActions.before: Tests organization.addresses[] via org query — plain address list
syncActions.after: Retained as-is: GQL-047 tests schema coverage of organization.addresses[], still a valid field for plain access. References appended 'Reviewed: VCST-4710 (2026-04-23)'. Automation_Status: unchanged.
syncActions.note: Same rationale as GQL-046. Organization.addresses not removed, only superseded for search use case by currentOrganizationAddresses.

syncedSuiteFiles.note: Phase 2 sync changes described above are NOT yet written back to the production regression CSVs (011, 013, 050d). They require a separate approval step before folding into the regression suites. Changes documented in syncActions above with before/after diffs for the approver to apply.

new.rule: Facet option labels in address selection UI must display human-readable values (e.g. city name, country name) and must NOT display internal field identifiers or postal codes. The `label` field from `term_facets[].terms[]` must be used for display, not `term`.
new.source: Defect #2 in VCST-4710 — City facet showed '10001' (postal code) instead of 'New York'; exposed that label vs term mapping was wrong in AddressSearchService facet aggregation

new.rule: Column headers in address selection table that declare `aria-sort` attribute must have a click handler and cursor:pointer style. Declaring `aria-sort='none'` without a click handler creates an accessibility contract violation and a broken sort UX promise.
new.source: Defect #1 in VCST-4710 — column headers had aria-sort='none' but no click handler wired

narrative: Phase 2 scan of 87 cases across 6 suites found 5 stale cases and 3 incomplete cases. The stale cases (CHK-003, CHK-035, CHK-059, CHK-060 in checkout suites; CHK-058 incomplete) all predate the new server-side search popup and referenced either legacy dropdown patterns or the legacy REST address endpoint. The two xProfile suite cases (GQL-046, GQL-047) were confirmed VALID because they test plain field access on legacy contact/organization address connections, which remain in the schema and are not removed. Phase 3 generated 30 cases targeting P0+P1 only from the BA report's 52 scenarios; 22 scenarios were deferred (sort P2 pending Defect #1 fix, perf P2 pending seed, PRESEL P2 pending OQ-10, Enter-key P2 pending OQ-3). All 3 new GraphQL queries (currentCustomerAddresses, currentOrganizationAddresses, checkDuplicateAddress) were confirmed present in the 2026-04-23 schema refresh before test case generation proceeded. Phase 4 review passed all structural gates. Two BL-proposal candidates were identified for domain-level invariants not currently captured in business-logic.md (facet label display rule, sort wiring rule). Four cases remain gated on external blockers: OQ-5 (seed 20+ addresses) blocks SF-012 and GQL-003/GQL-005/GQL-007; OQ-8 (Add New scope) blocks SF-020; Defect #1 fix blocks meaningful sort coverage. The Phase 2 sync write-back to production CSVs is staged but requires approver sign-off before the 5 changed cases are committed to the regression suites.
